Title: A GLUTAMATE CYSTEINE LIGASE Gene StGSH1 Regulated by StERF10 Enhanced Glutathione Accumulation and Adaptation to Low Phosphorus Stress in Potato

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rate-limiting first step of glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent ligation of L-glutamate and L-cysteine to form γ-glutamylcysteine, the first committed intermediate of glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate precursor of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the rate-limiting enzyme for GSH synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all analyzed organs, highest in leaves, followed by roots, stems, and tubers; induced by low phosphorus, detectable after 12 h and up to ~7-fold by 48 h; promoter active in leaf veins, crown cells, and root vascular tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves and roots; strongly induced by low-phosphorus treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Dual CaMV35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native StGSH1 promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi silencing; pharmacological inhibition with BSO; exogenous GSH feeding; recombinant enzyme assay; protein purification; crystallography; GFP subcellular localization; metabolite profiling; promoter-GUS analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-phosphorus stress (0.1 mM KH2PO4); exogenous GSH; BSO treatment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains redox homeostasis, scavenges ROS, alleviates oxidative damage, supports membrane lipid remodeling and adaptation to low-phosphorus stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glutathione accumulation, improves GSH/GSSG ratio, reduces H2O2, O2−, electrolyte leakage and MDA, enhances Pi remobilization-associated lipid remodeling, and improves potato adaptation to low-phosphorus stress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lines showed growth suppression, higher oxidative damage, impaired lipid remodeling, and lower low-P adaptation; no major total phosphorus acquisition differences were detected among genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target for engineering/breeding potato with improved phosphorus-use efficiency and low-phosphorus tolerance; can be combined with upstream regulator StERF10 for pathway-module enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purified protein formed both monomer and homodimer, with dimer more active than monomer; crystal structure showed canonical plant GCL fold and glutamate-binding pocket; likely supports sulfolipid synthesis indirectly by enabling sulfite provision for UDP-Sq synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StGSH1 encodes a functional plastid-localized glutamate-cysteine ligase and is the rate-limiting enzyme of GSH biosynthesis in potato. 2. Low phosphorus strongly induces StGSH1 expression, increasing glutathione accumulation. 3. Overexpression elevates GSH and LP tolerance, whereas RNAi silencing lowers GSH and aggravates oxidative and growth damage under LP. 4. StGSH1 promotes phospholipid-to-sulfolipid remodeling under LP, increasing SQDG and reducing PE. 5. StERF10 directly activates StGSH1 transcription through promoter binding.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in all analyzed organs with expression pattern similar to StGSH1; rapidly induced in leaves by low-phosphorus stress before StGSH1 induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves under low-phosphorus stress; induced early during LP treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression and GFP fusion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening and pairwise assay; dual-luciferase assay; GUS transactivation assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; overexpression; RNAi silencing; BSO inhibition; exogenous GSH rescue; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation by binding GCC-box in the StGSH1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Glutathione biosynthesis, redox homeostasis, ROS scavenging-associated responses, and membrane lipid remodeling under low phosphorus
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-phosphorus stress (0.1 mM KH2PO4); BSO treatment; exogenous GSH feeding
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains redox balance, reduces oxidative damage, supports low-phosphorus adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: LP-induced direct transcriptional activator that is a decisive upstream regulator of StGSH1-mediated glutathione accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes StGSH1 expression, increases GSH and GSH/GSSG ratio, reduces MDA and ROS, and enhances potato adaptation to low-phosphorus stress
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lines showed reduced shoot/root fresh weight, reduced GSH, and heightened oxidative damage under LP; no specific overexpression penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulatory target for breeding or engineering potato cultivars with improved phosphorus-use efficiency and low-phosphorus tolerance, especially in combination with StGSH1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among nine promoter-binding candidates, only StERF10 activated StGSH1 in transient assays; BSO reduced LP adaptation in StERF10-OE plants and exogenous GSH partially rescued StERF10-RNAi plants, supporting that StERF10 acts largely through StGSH1-mediated GSH biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StERF10 is an LP-induced AP2/ERF transcription factor expressed in organs similar to StGSH1 and localized in the nucleus. 2. It directly binds the GCC-box in the StGSH1 promoter, as shown by Y1H, EMSA, and ChIP-qPCR. 3. StERF10 activates StGSH1 transcription in dual-LUC and GUS assays. 4. Overexpression increases GSH accumulation and LP tolerance, while RNAi decreases GSH and worsens LP damage. 5. Pharmacological inhibition and rescue experiments show that StERF10 enhances LP adaptation largely in a GSH-dependent manner via StGSH1.
